articles.title: Depressed elderly adults are almost 5 times more likely to develop Alzheimer’s
articles.summary: Older adults diagnosed with depression face a severe risk of cognitive decline. A new longitudinal study reveals that depressed elderly individuals are nearly five times more likely to develop Alzheimer’s disease than their non-depressed peers.

articles.title: Relying on AI chatbots for historical facts can influence your political beliefs, new study shows
articles.summary: A recent study in PNAS Nexus suggests that reading history summaries generated by artificial intelligence can subtly shift your political opinions. Even when factually accurate, chatbots carry hidden biases that influence how people view the world.
articles.titleKo: 역사적 사실에 대해 AI 챗봇에 의존하는 것은 정치적 신념에 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다. 새로운 연구 결과
articles.aiSummary: A recent study in PNAS Nexus suggests that reading history summaries generated by artificial intelligence can subtly shift your political opinions. Even when factually accurate, chatbots carry hidden biases that influence how people view the world.

articles.title: Bitboard version of Tetris AI
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.26765v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The efficiency of game engines and policy optimization algorithms is crucial for training reinforcement learning (RL) agents in complex sequential decision-making tasks, such as Tetris. Existing Tetris implementations suffer from low simulation speeds,
articles.titleKo: 테트리스 AI의 비트보드 버전
articles.aiSummary: arXiv:2603.26765v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The efficiency of game engines and policy optimization algorithms is crucial for training reinforcement learning (RL) agents in complex sequential decision-making tasks, such as Tetris. Existing Tetris implementations suffer from low simulation speeds, suboptimal state evaluation, and inefficient training paradigms, limiting their utility for large-scale RL research. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a high-performance Tetris AI framework based on bitboard optimization and improved RL algorithms. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework enhances Tetris's utility as an RL benchmark by bridging low-level bitboard optimizations with high-level AI strategies, providing a sample-efficient and computationally lightweight solution for scalable sequential decision-making research.
articles.aiSummaryKo: arXiv: 2603.26765v1 발표 유형: NEW 요약: 게임 엔진 및 정책 최적화 알고리즘의 효율성은 테트리스와 같은 복잡한 순차적 의사 결정 작업에서 강화 학습 (RL) 에이전트를 교육하는 데 중요합니다. 기존 테트리스 구현은 낮은 시뮬레이션 속도, 차선의 상태 평가 및 비효율적인 교육 패러다임으로 인해 대규모 테트리스에 대한 유용성이 제한됩니다.

To address these limitations, this paper proposes a high-performance Tetris AI framework based on bitboard optimization and improved RL algorithms. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework enhances Tetris's utility as an RL benchmark by bridging low-level bitboard optimizations with high-level AI strategies, providing a sample-efficient and computationally lightweight solution for scalable sequential decision-making research.

articles.title: Multiverse: Language-Conditioned Multi-Game Level Blending via Shared Representation
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.26782v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Text-to-level generation aims to translate natural language descriptions into structured game levels, enabling intuitive control over procedural content generation. While prior text-to-level generators are typically limited to a single game domain, ext
articles.titleKo: 멀티버스: 공유 표현을 통한 언어 컨디셔닝 멀티 게임 레벨 블렌딩
articles.aiSummary: arXiv:2603.26782v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Text-to-level generation aims to translate natural language descriptions into structured game levels, enabling intuitive control over procedural content generation. While prior text-to-level generators are typically limited to a single game domain, extending language-conditioned generation to multiple games requires learning representations that capture structural relationships across domains. The model learns a shared latent space aligning textual instructions and level structures, while a threshold-based multi-positive contrastive supervision links semantically related levels across games. Experiments show that the learned representation supports controllable cross-game level blending and significantly improves blending quality within the same game genre, while providing a unified representation for language-conditioned multi-game content generation.
articles.aiSummaryKo: arXiv: 2603.26782v1 발표 유형: NEW 요약: 텍스트 대 레벨 생성은 자연어 설명을 구조화된 게임 레벨로 변환하여 절차적 콘텐츠 생성에 대한 직관적인 제어를 가능하게 하는 것을 목표로 합니다. 이전의 텍스트 대 레벨 생성기는 일반적으로 단일 게임 도메인으로 제한되지만, 언어 조건 생성을 여러 게임으로 확장하려면

Experiments show that the learned representation supports controllable cross-game level blending and significantly improves blending quality within the same game genre, while providing a unified representation for language-conditioned multi-game content generation.

articles.title: Concerning Uncertainty -- A Systematic Survey of Uncertainty-Aware XAI
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.26838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper surveys uncertainty-aware explainable artificial intelligence (UAXAI), examining how uncertainty is incorporated into explanatory pipelines and how such methods are evaluated. Across the literature, three recurring approaches to uncertainty
articles.titleKo: 불확실성 관련 -- 불확실성을 인식하는 XAI에 대한 체계적인 조사
articles.aiSummary: arXiv:2603.26838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper surveys uncertainty-aware explainable artificial intelligence (UAXAI), examining how uncertainty is incorporated into explanatory pipelines and how such methods are evaluated. Across the literature, three recurring approaches to uncertainty quantification emerge (Bayesian, Monte Carlo, and Conformal methods), alongside distinct strategies for integrating uncertainty into explanations: assessing trustworthiness, constraining models or explanations, and explicitly communicating uncertainty. Evaluation practices remain fragmented and largely model centered, with limited attention to users and inconsistent reporting of reliability properties (e.g., calibration, coverage, explanation stability). Recent work leans towards calibration, distribution free techniques and recognizes explainer variability as a central concern.
articles.aiSummaryKo: arXiv: 2603.26838v1 발표 유형: NEW Abstract: 본 논문은 불확실성을 인식하는 설명 가능한 인공 지능 (UAXAI) 을 조사하여 불확실성이 설명 파이프 라인에 어떻게 통합되는지, 그리고 그러한 방법이 어떻게 평가되는지를 조사합니다. 문헌 전반에 걸쳐 불확실성 정량화에 대한 세 가지 반복적 접근법 (베이지안, 몬테카를로 및 등각 방법) 이 뚜렷한 전략과 함께 등장합니다.
articles.aiSummaryEn: arXiv:2603.26838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper surveys uncertainty-aware explainable artificial intelligence (UAXAI), examining how uncertainty is incorporated into explanatory pipelines and how such methods are evaluated. Evaluation practices remain fragmented and largely model centered, with limited attention to users and inconsistent reporting of reliability properties (e.g., calibration, coverage, explanation stability). Recent work leans towards calibration, distribution free techniques and recognizes explainer variability as a central concern.

articles.title: Neuro-Symbolic Learning for Predictive Process Monitoring via Two-Stage Logic Tensor Networks with Rule Pruning
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.26944v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Predictive modeling on sequential event data is critical for fraud detection and healthcare monitoring. Existing data-driven approaches learn correlations from historical data but fail to incorporate domain-specific sequential constraints and logical r
articles.titleKo: 규칙 정리 기능이 있는 2단계 논리 텐서 네트워크를 통한 예측 프로세스 모니터링을 위한 신경 기호 학습
articles.aiSummary: arXiv:2603.26944v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Predictive modeling on sequential event data is critical for fraud detection and healthcare monitoring. Existing data-driven approaches learn correlations from historical data but fail to incorporate domain-specific sequential constraints and logical rules governing event relationships, limiting accuracy and regulatory compliance. Evaluation on four real-world event logs shows that domain knowledge injection significantly improves predictive performance, with the two-stage optimization proving essential knowledge (without it, knowledge can severely degrade performance). The approach excels particularly in compliance-constrained scenarios with limited compliant training examples, achieving superior performance compared to purely data-driven baselines while ensuring adherence to domain constraints.
articles.aiSummaryKo: arXiv: 2603.26944v1 발표 유형: NEW 요약: 순차적 이벤트 데이터에 대한 예측 모델링은 사기 탐지 및 의료 모니터링에 매우 중요합니다. 기존 데이터 기반 접근 방식은 과거 데이터로부터 상관 관계를 학습하지만 이벤트 관계를 관리하는 도메인별 순차적 제약 및 논리적 규칙을 통합하지 못하여 정확성과 규제 준수를 제한합니다. 4가지에 대한 평가

articles.title: Mitigating Forgetting in Continual Learning with Selective Gradient Projection
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.26671v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As neural networks are increasingly deployed in dynamic environments, they face the challenge of catastrophic forgetting, the tendency to overwrite previously learned knowledge when adapting to new tasks, resulting in severe performance degradation on
articles.titleKo: 선택적 그라디언트 투영법을 사용하여 지속적인 학습에서 잊기 완화
articles.aiSummary: arXiv:2603.26671v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As neural networks are increasingly deployed in dynamic environments, they face the challenge of catastrophic forgetting, the tendency to overwrite previously learned knowledge when adapting to new tasks, resulting in severe performance degradation on earlier tasks. We propose Selective Forgetting-Aware Optimization (SFAO), a dynamic method that regulates gradient directions via cosine similarity and per-layer gating, enabling controlled forgetting while balancing plasticity and stability. SFAO selectively projects, accepts, or discards updates using a tunable mechanism with efficient Monte Carlo approximation. Experiments on standard continual learning benchmarks show that SFAO achieves competitive accuracy with markedly lower memory cost, a 90$\%$ reduction, and improved forgetting on MNIST datasets, making it suitable for resource-constrained scenarios.
articles.aiSummaryKo: arXiv: 2603.26671v1 발표 유형: NEW 개요: 신경망이 동적 환경에 점점 더 많이 배치됨에 따라, 새로운 작업에 적응할 때 이전에 배운 지식을 덮어쓰는 경향인 치명적인 망각의 도전에 직면하여 이전 작업에서 심각한 성능 저하를 초래합니다. 우리는 선택적 망각 인식 최적화 (Selective Forgetting-Aware Optimization, SFAO) 를 제안합니다.

SFAO selectively projects, accepts, or discards updates using a tunable mechanism with efficient Monte Carlo approximation. Experiments on standard continual learning benchmarks show that SFAO achieves competitive accuracy with markedly lower memory cost, a 90$\%$ reduction, and improved forgetting on MNIST datasets, making it suitable for resource-constrained scenarios.

articles.title: Boundary-aware Prototype-driven Adversarial Alignment for Cross-Corpus EEG Emotion Recognition
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.26713v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG)-based emotion recognition suffers from severe performance degradation when models are transferred across heterogeneous datasets due to physiological variability, experimental paradigm differences, and device inconsistencies
articles.titleKo: 교차 코퍼스 뇌파 감정 인식을 위한 경계 인식 프로토타입 기반 적대적 정렬
articles.aiSummary: arXiv:2603.26713v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG)-based emotion recognition suffers from severe performance degradation when models are transferred across heterogeneous datasets due to physiological variability, experimental paradigm differences, and device inconsistencies. Existing domain adversarial methods primarily enforce global marginal alignment and often overlook class-conditional mismatch and decision boundary distortion, limiting cross-corpus generalization. By combining prototype-guided subdomain alignment, contrastive discriminative enhancement, and boundary-aware aggregation within a coherent adversarial architecture, the proposed framework reformulates emotion recognition as a relation-driven representation learning problem, reducing sensitivity to label noise and improving cross-domain stability. Extensive experiments on SEED, SEED-IV, and SEED-V demonstrate state-of-the-art performance under four cross-corpus evaluation protocols, with average improvements of 6.72\%, 5.59\%, 6.69\%, and 4.83\%, respectively.
articles.aiSummaryKo: arXiv: 2603.26713v1 발표 유형: NEW 개요: 뇌파 (EEG) 기반 감정 인식은 생리적 가변성, 실험적 패러다임 차이 및 장치 불일치로 인해 모델이 이질적인 데이터 세트에 걸쳐 전송될 때 심각한 성능 저하를 겪습니다. 기존 도메인 적대적 방법은 주로 전역 한계 정렬을 시행하고 종종 간과합니다

articles.title: Learning to Select Visual In-Context Demonstrations
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.26775v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) adapt to visual tasks via in-context learning (ICL), which relies heavily on demonstration quality. The dominant demonstration selection strategy is unsupervised k-Nearest Neighbor (kNN) search. While simple, th
articles.titleKo: 시각적 직접 시연 선택 방법 배우기
articles.aiSummary: arXiv:2603.26775v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) adapt to visual tasks via in-context learning (ICL), which relies heavily on demonstration quality. While simple, this similarity-first approach is sub-optimal for complex factual regression tasks; it selects redundant examples that fail to capture the task's full output range. We reframe selection as a sequential decision-making problem and introduce Learning to Select Demonstrations (LSD), training a Reinforcement Learning agent to construct optimal demonstration sets. Evaluating across five visual regression benchmarks, we uncover a crucial dichotomy: while kNN remains optimal for subjective preference tasks, LSD significantly outperforms baselines on objective, factual regression tasks.
articles.aiSummaryKo: arXiv: 2603.26775v1 발표 유형: NEW 요약: MLLM (Multimodal Large Language Models) 은 데모 품질에 크게 의존하는 컨텍스트 내 학습 (ICL) 을 통해 시각적 작업에 적응합니다. 간단하지만, 이 유사성 우선 접근법은 복잡한 사실 회귀 작업에 대해 차선책입니다. 작업의 전체 출력 범위를 캡처하지 못하는 중복 예제를 선택합니다. 우리는 선택을 시퀀스로 재구성합니다

While simple, this similarity-first approach is sub-optimal for complex factual regression tasks; it selects redundant examples that fail to capture the task's full output range. We reframe selection as a sequential decision-making problem and introduce Learning to Select Demonstrations (LSD), training a Reinforcement Learning agent to construct optimal demonstration sets. Evaluating across five visual regression benchmarks, we uncover a crucial dichotomy: while kNN remains optimal for subjective preference tasks, LSD significantly outperforms baselines on objective, factual regression tasks.

articles.title: TED: Training-Free Experience Distillation for Multimodal Reasoning
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.26778v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge distillation is typically realized by transferring a teacher model's knowledge into a student's parameters through supervised or reinforcement-based optimization. While effective, such approaches require repeated parameter updates and large-s
articles.titleKo: TED: 멀티모달 추론을 위한 훈련 없는 경험 증류
articles.aiSummary: arXiv:2603.26778v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge distillation is typically realized by transferring a teacher model's knowledge into a student's parameters through supervised or reinforcement-based optimization. While effective, such approaches require repeated parameter updates and large-scale training data, limiting their applicability in resource-constrained environments. In this work, we propose TED, a training-free, context-based distillation framework that shifts the update target of distillation from model parameters to an in-context experience injected into the student's prompt. The teacher then compares the student trajectories with its reasoning and the ground-truth answer, extracting generalized experiences that capture effective reasoning patterns.
articles.aiSummaryKo: arXiv: 2603.26778v1 발표 유형: NEW 요약: 지식 증류는 일반적으로 감독 또는 강화 기반 최적화를 통해 교사 모델의 지식을 학생의 매개 변수로 전달함으로써 실현됩니다. 이러한 접근법은 효과적이지만 반복적인 매개 변수 업데이트와 대규모 교육 데이터가 필요하므로 리소스가 제한된 환경에서의 적용이 제한됩니다. 이 작업에서는

articles.summary: arXiv:2603.27347v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantitative estimation of information flow in early vision with psychophysically realistic networks is still an open issue. This is because, up to date, the necessary elements (general and plausible network, accurate noise, and reliable information me
articles.titleKo: 현실적인 잡음, 피드백 및 비선형성을 가진 반복적인 Retina-V1 네트워크의 정보
articles.aiSummary: As a result, previous works made different approximations that limit the generality of their results. (3) Reliable information measures that have been checked with analytical results, both in general [IEEE PAMI 24], and in similar visual neuroscience contexts [Neurocomp.24], and hence can be applied in this (more general) case where analytical results are difficult to obtain. The joint use of these elements allows a reliable study of information flow depending on different connectivity schemes (different nonlinearities and interactions), different noise sources, and different stimuli. Results show the benefits of feedback in two ways: (1) the information loss in the data processing inequality along the pathway is reduced by the V1 -- > LGN recurrence for values of feedback that give stable steady state solutions, and (2) the stability of the net is assessed though standard Poincar\'e analysis and we find an optimal value for the feedback in terms of the accuracy of the reconstructed signal from the cortical representation.

articles.title: Grounding Social Perception in Intuitive Physics
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.27410v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: People infer rich social information from others' actions. These inferences are often constrained by the physical world: what agents can do, what obstacles permit, and how the physical actions of agents causally change an environment and other agents'
articles.titleKo: 직관적 물리학에 대한 사회적 인식의 기초
articles.aiSummary: These inferences are often constrained by the physical world: what agents can do, what obstacles permit, and how the physical actions of agents causally change an environment and other agents' mental states and behavior. We then present a computational model, SIMPLE, a physics-grounded Bayesian inverse planning model that integrates planning, probabilistic planning, and physics simulation to infer agents' goals and relations from their trajectories. Our experimental results showed that SIMPLE achieved high accuracy and agreement with human judgments across diverse scenarios, while feedforward baseline models -- including strong vision-language models -- and physics-agnostic inverse planning failed to achieve human-level performance and did not align with human judgments. These results suggest that our model provides a computational account for how people understand physically grounded social scenes by inverting a generative model of physics and agents.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 이러한 추론은 종종 물리적 세계에 의해 제약을 받습니다. 즉, 상담원이 할 수 있는 일, 장애물이 허용하는 일, 상담원의 물리적 행동이 환경과 다른 상담원의 정신 상태 및 행동을 어떻게 변화시키는지에 대한 것입니다. 그런 다음 계획, 확률 계획 및 물리 시뮬레이션을 통합하여 추론하는 간단한 물리학 기반 베이지안 역계획 모델을 제시합니다.
articles.aiSummaryEn: These inferences are often constrained by the physical world: what agents can do, what obstacles permit, and how the physical actions of agents causally change an environment and other agents' mental states and behavior. We then present a computational model, SIMPLE, a physics-grounded Bayesian inverse planning model that integrates planning, probabilistic planning, and physics simulation to infer agents' goals and relations from their trajectories. Our experimental results showed that SIMPLE achieved high accuracy and agreement with human judgments across diverse scenarios, while feedforward baseline models -- including strong vision-language models -- and physics-agnostic inverse planning failed to achieve human-level performance and did not align with human judgments. These results suggest that our model provides a computational account for how people understand physically grounded social scenes by inverting a generative model of physics and agents.

articles.title: Energy Landscapes of Emotion: Quantifying Brain Network Stability During Happy and Sad Face Processing Using EEG-Based Hopfield Energy
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.27644v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Understanding how the human brain instantiates distinct emotional states is a key challenge in affective neuroscience. While network-based approaches have advanced emotion processing research,they remain largely descriptive,leaving the dynamical stabil
articles.titleKo: 감정의 에너지 풍경: 뇌파 기반 홉필드 에너지를 사용하여 행복하고 슬픈 얼굴 처리 중 뇌 네트워크 안정성 정량화
articles.aiSummary: While network-based approaches have advanced emotion processing research,they remain largely descriptive,leaving the dynamical stability of emotional brain states unquantified.This study introduces a novel framework to quantify this stability by applying Hopfield network energy to empirically derived functional connectivity. Functional connectivity was estimated using the weighted Phase Lag Index to obtain artifact-robust,frequency-specific matrices, which served as coupling weights in a continuous Hopfield energy model to calculate a scalar energy value per trial. Statistical comparisons showed sad emotional processing was associated with significantly lower(more negative) energy in delta,theta,and alpha bands,with the strongest effect in the alpha band (Cohen's d =0.83). Energy correlated strongly negatively with global efficiency(r=-0.72),indicating hyperconnected,efficient networks correspond to more stable states.Additionally, alpha-band energy correlated positively with reaction time during sad trials(r=0.61),linking deeper network stability to increased cognitive effort.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 네트워크 기반 접근법은 정서 처리 연구가 발전했지만, 대부분 설명력이 남아있어 감정적 뇌 상태의 역동적 안정성은 정량화되지 않았습니다. 이 연구는 Hopfield 네트워크 에너지를 경험적으로 도출된 기능적 연결성에 적용하여 이러한 안정성을 정량화하기 위한 새로운 프레임워크를 소개합니다. 기능적 연결성은 가중된 위상 지연 지수를 사용하여 추정되었습니다.
articles.aiSummaryEn: While network-based approaches have advanced emotion processing research,they remain largely descriptive,leaving the dynamical stability of emotional brain states unquantified.This study introduces a novel framework to quantify this stability by applying Hopfield network energy to empirically derived functional connectivity. Functional connectivity was estimated using the weighted Phase Lag Index to obtain artifact-robust,frequency-specific matrices, which served as coupling weights in a continuous Hopfield energy model to calculate a scalar energy value per trial. Statistical comparisons showed sad emotional processing was associated with significantly lower(more negative) energy in delta,theta,and alpha bands,with the strongest effect in the alpha band (Cohen's d =0.83). Energy correlated strongly negatively with global efficiency(r=-0.72),indicating hyperconnected,efficient networks correspond to more stable states.Additionally, alpha-band energy correlated positively with reaction time during sad trials(r=0.61),linking deeper network stability to increased cognitive effort.

articles.title: Allocentric Navigation Is Computationally Universal
articles.summary: arXiv:2603.27926v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This report presents three proofs showing that idealized architectures capable of navigation guided by allocentric maps with landmark structure can be computationally universal. The navigation may occur either online (in the environment) or offline (in
articles.titleKo: Allocentric Navigation은 Computationally Universal입니다.
articles.aiSummary: arXiv:2603.27926v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This report presents three proofs showing that idealized architectures capable of navigation guided by allocentric maps with landmark structure can be computationally universal. These constructions are mathematically close to classical graph-based models in computability theory, including Kolmogorov-Uspensky machines, storage-modification machines, graph Turing machines, and related navigation-on-graphs models. Accordingly, the bare universality results are mathematically unsurprising. Nevertheless, the present treatment is, as far as I know, the first self-contained reconstruction of such universality demonstrations in the idiom of allocentric cognitive maps and offline navigation, that is, within an architecture whose core representational and computational primitives are drawn from a body of empirical and theoretical work on spatial navigation.
articles.aiSummaryKo: arXiv: 2603.27926v1 발표 유형: NEW 요약: 이 보고서는 랜드마크 구조의 할당 중심 맵에 의해 안내되는 탐색이 가능한 이상화된 아키텍처가 계산적으로 보편적일 수 있음을 보여주는 세 가지 증거를 제시합니다. 이러한 구조는 Kolmogorov-Uspensky 기계, 스토리지 수정 기계, g를 포함한 계산 가능성 이론의 고전적인 그래프 기반 모델에 수학적으로 가깝습니다.

articles.title: ‘Looking Backward’ to the Future
articles.summary: I first saw the light in the city of Boston in the year 1857. “What!” you say. “Eighteen fifty-seven? That is an odd slip. He means nineteen fifty-seven, of course.” I beg pardon, but there is no mistake. It was about four in the afternoon of December the 26th, one day after Christmas, in the year 1
articles.titleKo: 미래를 ’뒤돌아보기'
articles.aiSummary: The man who had accomplished this, and it was the end all sought, was said to live on the income of his investments. To explain at this point how the ancient methods of industry made this possible would delay us too much. It must not be supposed that an arrangement which seems so unnatural and preposterous according to modern notions was never criticized by your ancestors. It must in truth be admitted that the main effect of the spectacle of the misery of the toilers at the rope was to enhance the passengers' sense of the value of their seats upon the coach, and to cause them to hold on to them more desperately than before.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 이것을 성취한 남자는, 그리고 그것은 모두 추구하는 끝이었고, 그의 투자의 수입으로 살았다고 한다. 이 시점에서 고대의 산업 방법이 어떻게 이것을 가능하게 만들었는지 설명하는 것은 우리를 너무 늦출 것입니다. 현대의 관념에 따라 그렇게 부자연스럽고 터무니없는 것처럼 보이는 배열이 조상들에 의해 결코 비판받지 않았다고 가정해서는 안됩니다. 실제로는 admi여야 합니다.

articles.title: Facts and the Fight for Moral High Ground
articles.summary: In southwest Philadelphia in August 2019, a young Black man sat down on a folding chair in a high school auditorium and clipped a lapel mic to a bright yellow T-shirt that read “Philly Thrive.” “So, how you doin’? My name is Ricky,” he said into a camera. “I’m a resident in the Gray’s Ferry communit
articles.titleKo: 사실과 도덕적 고지를 위한 싸움
articles.aiSummary: In addition to these arguments over moral standards, participants in the Story Station were fighting over who deserved to be protected, who deserved to be defended against wrongs, and who deserved to be listened to about matters of right and wrong. Somehow, all of the research that has been done hasn’t been very effective in shifting the standards to which we hold industrial facilities. A white woman with decades of experience in her field, Howarth started by introducing her organization and its mission: “The environmental health researchers, physicians, and public health professionals of the CEET work every day on environmental health issues that affect our region and recognize the value of scientific evidence to establish and maintain public policy protective of human health.” From this you might glean that Howarth and CEET were on the side of community members like Sylvia Bennett and Sonya Sanders, who believed that illnesses in the Gray’s Ferry neighborhood were connected to pollution from the refinery. The report then invoked another set of public health researchers, in this case from Drexel University, citing their claim that exposures to air pollutants increased respiratory disease among people living near refineries in particular.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 도덕적 기준에 대한 이러한 주장 외에도 스토리 스테이션의 참가자들은 보호받을 자격이 있는 사람, 잘못에 대해 방어받을 자격이 있는 사람, 옳고 그름의 문제에 대해 경청할 자격이 있는 사람을 놓고 싸우고 있었습니다. 어쨌든, 수행된 모든 연구는 우리가 산업 시설을 보유한 표준을 전환하는 데 그다지 효과적이지 않았습니다. A white wo
articles.aiSummaryEn: In addition to these arguments over moral standards, participants in the Story Station were fighting over who deserved to be protected, who deserved to be defended against wrongs, and who deserved to be listened to about matters of right and wrong. Somehow, all of the research that has been done hasn’t been very effective in shifting the standards to which we hold industrial facilities. A white woman with decades of experience in her field, Howarth started by introducing her organization and its mission: “The environmental health researchers, physicians, and public health professionals of the CEET work every day on environmental health issues that affect our region and recognize the value of scientific evidence to establish and maintain public policy protective of human health.” From this you might glean that Howarth and CEET were on the side of community members like Sylvia Bennett and Sonya Sanders, who believed that illnesses in the Gray’s Ferry neighborhood were connected to pollution from the refinery. The report then invoked another set of public health researchers, in this case from Drexel University, citing their claim that exposures to air pollutants increased respiratory disease among people living near refineries in particular.

What is the one sound on the crew radio that stops every lineman cold? And why d

articles.title: What It Takes to Make Good Decisions: Judgment, Not Calculation
articles.summary: Consider the following. A young child, having received a playhouse as a gift and finding its interior too dark, asks her mother how she makes their house so light. “By flipping a switch,” says the mother. The child finds a spare switch in the basement, hangs it on the playhouse wall, and flips it, b
articles.titleKo: 올바른 결정을 내리기 위해 필요한 것: 계산이 아닌 판단
articles.aiSummary: It is also a paradigm in the sense used by philosopher Thomas Kuhn in his landmark book The Structure of Scientific Revolutions, in which he suggests that scientific paradigms establish both the problems to be solved and the methods of inquiry to be used in formulating a solution to those problems. The same is true of most other significant decisions in life. By keeping background information skeletal, researchers make decision problems seem more similar to one another than they really are, and more simple than they really are. Quantification can turn any decision into a “no-brainer.” But making decisions is what brains are for.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 또한 철학자 토마스 쿤 (Thomas Kuhn) 이 그의 획기적인 저서인 과학 혁명의 구조 (The Structure of Scientific Revolutions) 에서 사용하는 의미의 패러다임으로, 과학 패러다임이 해결해야 할 문제와 그 문제에 대한 해결책을 수립하는 데 사용할 탐구 방법을 모두 확립할 것을 제안합니다. 인생에서 다른 중요한 결정들도 마찬가지입니다. 배경 정보 스켈을 유지하여

articles.title: Scientists discover sleep switch that builds muscle, burns fat, and boosts brainpower
articles.summary: Deep sleep does far more than rest the body — it activates a powerful brain-driven system that controls growth hormone, fueling muscle and bone strength, metabolism, and even mental performance. Scientists have now mapped the neural circuits behind this process, uncovering a delicate feedback loop i
articles.titleKo: 과학자들은 근육을 키우고, 지방을 태우고, 두뇌 능력을 향상시키는 수면 스위치를 발견합니다.
articles.aiSummary: Deep sleep does far more than rest the body — it activates a powerful brain-driven system that controls growth hormone, fueling muscle and bone strength, metabolism, and even mental performance. Scientists have now mapped the neural circuits behind this process, uncovering a delicate feedback loop in which sleep boosts growth hormone, and that same hormone helps regulate wakefulness.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 깊은 수면은 신체를 쉬게 하는 것 이상의 역할을 합니다. 성장 호르몬을 조절하는 강력한 뇌 중심 시스템을 활성화하여 근육과 뼈의 힘, 신진대사, 심지어 정신적 능력을 향상시킵니다. 과학자들은 이제 이 과정의 신경 회로를 매핑하여 수면이 성장 호르몬을 증가시키는 섬세한 피드백 루프를 발견했으며, 동일한 호르몬이 각성을 조절하는 데 도움이 됩니다.
articles.aiSummaryEn: Deep sleep does far more than rest the body — it activates a powerful brain-driven system that controls growth hormone, fueling muscle and bone strength, metabolism, and even mental performance. Scientists have now mapped the neural circuits behind this process, uncovering a delicate feedback loop in which sleep boosts growth hormone, and that same hormone helps regulate wakefulness.

articles.title: Just a few minutes of effort could lower your risk of 8 major diseases
articles.summary: Just a few minutes of getting out of breath each day could dramatically cut your risk of major diseases—including heart disease, dementia, and diabetes. A large study of nearly 100,000 people found that it’s not just how much you move, but how intensely you move that matters. Short bursts of vigorou
articles.titleKo: 몇 분만 노력하면 8가지 주요 질병의 위험을 낮출 수 있습니다.
articles.aiSummary: Just a few minutes of getting out of breath each day could dramatically cut your risk of major diseases—including heart disease, dementia, and diabetes. A large study of nearly 100,000 people found that it’s not just how much you move, but how intensely you move that matters. Short bursts of vigorous activity—like rushing for a bus or climbing stairs quickly—were linked to striking reductions in disease risk, especially for inflammatory conditions and brain health.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 매일 몇 분만 숨을 쉬면 심장 질환, 치매 및 당뇨병을 포함한 주요 질병의 위험을 크게 줄일 수 있습니다. 거의 10만 명에 달하는 사람들을 대상으로 한 대규모 연구에 따르면, 얼마나 많이 움직이는지가 중요한 것이 아니라 얼마나 강렬하게 움직이는지가 중요한 것으로 나타났습니다. 버스를 타러 달려가거나 계단을 빨리 오르는 것과 같은 격렬한 활동의 짧은 파열은 d의 눈에 띄는 감소와 연결되었습니다.
